title: The Kohli Chase
subtitle: Inspired by India v Pakistan — T20 World Cup 2022, Melbourne Cricket Ground
story: 90,293 fans at the MCG. Pakistan post a defendable total after a late collapse, then reduce India to 31/4 inside seven overs. What follows is widely called the greatest T20I innings ever played — Virat Kohli's unbeaten 82 off 53 and a final over of pure chaos. Play this arc with YOUR teams: a pegged-back first innings, a top-order collapse in the chase, a long rebuild, and a mountain to climb at the death. The match always reaches the same crossroads — how it ends is up to the simulation.
tagline: Same collapse. Same pressure. Your teams, a different ending every time.

1.note: New-ball strikes — the top order under siege
A patient rebuild from the wreckage
The platform is set for a late launch
Death-overs squeeze pegs the total back

2.note: The collapse — four down and the chase in ruins
Two batters dig in as the asking rate climbs
A mountain to climb in the last three overs
